rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.reason: Declared source-domain Wikipedia page on khat/qat. Matches the source-side content word 'yemeni mafraj qat session' (kw=1, below 2-threshold). No LLM mechanism; sem and func far below thresholds.

Qwen Gated Attention (NeurIPS 2025 Best Paper) introduces an attention-gating module (value-projection non-linearity, sparsity, no attention sink). Directly the attention-gating primitive that MAFRAJ-GATE rebrands as 'event-triggered hard cyclic gate'. sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7.

In-context KV-Cache Eviction via Attention-Gate: per-token eviction flags emitted by a gate module produce dynamic KV-cache clearing -- functionally same end-state as 'attention zeroed outside gate window + KV forcibly cleared'. sem>=0.7 and func>=0.7.

SWAT trains sliding-window attention to bound attention to a windowed region. The boundary trigger is positional rather than external-event, but the bounded-attention-window primitive is the same. sem and func at/above 0.7.
Persistent agent memory architecture as alternative to long-context. Different artefact (long-term store rather than gated attention window). Both sem and func below 0.7.
Empirical study of maximum effective context-window in LLMs. No gating or KV-clearing mechanism; descriptive only. Both sem and func below 0.7.

Adaptive Attention-Gate KV eviction: same primitive of bounded-attention-via-gate with adaptive trigger -- precisely the 'event-triggered gate-open/close cycle + KV clear' described in the candidate. sem and func both >= 0.7.
KV-cache offloading and inter-session reset patterns -- direct prior art for the candidate's 'forced inter-session KV clear' component. sem and func at/above 0.7.

fail_reason: 5 prior-art hits found (Qwen Gated Attention, Attention-Gate KV eviction, SWAT, Adaptive Attention-Gate eviction, BentoML KV-cache offloading/reset). MAFRAJ-GATE's three claimed components -- (a) hard event-bounded attention gate, (b) scheduled mid-session attention-temperature peak, and (c) forced inter-session KV clear -- are each individually covered by 2024-2026 attention-gating + KV-eviction + KV-offload literature. The only un-matched element is the cultural/temporal trigger framing (prayer-bounded window + Solomon's-hour peak), which is decorative and does not change the underlying LLM mechanism. total_hits>=1 triggers FAIL.
